mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real personal spending and housing starts indicate robust household demand and construction activity, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in spending and housing starts fails to produce a positive 60-day return direction in at least 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating short-term positive market momentum during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of expansion-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Markets record positive 20-day returns in over 65% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combination of payroll growth and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a directional accuracy above 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, consistently forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward return direction, with a Rank IC persistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and stagflationary pressures, triggering immediate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of periods characterized by flat industrial output and accelerating CPI.
